title: Colysis × shintenensis
intro: Colysis × shintenensis is a unique species of fern, characterized by its distinctive frond and habitat. The health benefits of Colysis × shintenensis are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to potentially alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Colysis × shintenensis is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Colysis × shintenensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a hybrid of the genera Colysis and Oreogrammitis. More specifically, it is classified within the family Polypodiaceae and the subfamily Polypodiodeae. Colysis × shintenensis is native to Japan, where it grows in shaded, humid environments. This hybrid fern is often cultivated for its unique foliage and is valued by botanists and horticulturists for its scientific and ornamental significance.
